messages.content: Build a pure Decider for a Release aggregate in TypeScript. Support plan, approve, and rollout commands. Approving a cancelled release must throw a typed DomainError. Export initialState, decide, and evolve with an exhaustive never default.
Write a Decider for a Deployment aggregate covering start, succeed, and rollback transitions. Rolling back a succeeded deployment must throw. Include evolve with exhaustive never default.
Explain briefly how a Decider handles an Incident aggregate, then implement one in TypeScript with open, acknowledge, and resolve transitions using strict types.
Build a Decider for an Alert aggregate with raise, suppress, and clear transitions. Clearing a suppressed alert must throw. Use discriminated unions and export initialState, decide, evolve.
Implement a pure Decider for a Membership aggregate in TypeScript with strict types. Commands: enroll, upgrade, expire. Upgrading an expired membership must throw a typed error.
Write a Decider for a Renewal aggregate covering request, confirm, and decline. Confirming a declined renewal must throw a typed error. Exhaustive never defaults required.

Write a TypeScript projection that folds a Pipeline event stream into a flat read model with the pipeline id, stage count, and last status. Use a project() function that ignores events outside its interest.
Build a Runbook read-model projection in TypeScript. The view summarises id, title, and step count. project() should return the view unchanged for events it doesn't care about.
How do I write a projection that tracks Cluster node counts? Show a minimal TypeScript example with project() and an initial empty view.
Write a TypeScript projection for OncallShift events producing a read model with engineer id and total minutes on call. project() must ignore non-shift events.
Explain briefly how a versioned snapshot upgrade works for a Release aggregate, then implement upgradeSnapshot in TypeScript with v1 to v3 upgraders.
Build a TypeScript Snapshot loader for a Deployment aggregate with CURRENT_VERSION = 3, and two upgrader functions v1Tov2 and v2Tov3.
Implement a Snapshot serialiser and loader for an Alert aggregate in TypeScript. Provide CURRENT_VERSION, two upgraders, and a load function that asserts the current shape.
Write a TypeScript write-path for an Incident aggregate that uses optimistic concurrency via expectedRevision. Surface a typed ConcurrencyConflict when the store reports a mismatch.
Write a TypeScript appendToStream wrapper for a Membership aggregate that uses expectedRevision for optimistic concurrency control. Include a local EventStore interface and ConcurrencyConflict error.
What's the TypeScript way to append Renewal events with optimistic concurrency against the current stream revision? Show a minimal example with EventStore and expectedRevision.
Build a TypeScript CommandHandler that loads a Release stream, decides a command, and appends new events with expectedRevision. Use a local EventStore interface.
Write a TypeScript CommandHandler for the Pipeline aggregate. It loads from readStream, runs decide, and calls appendToStream with expected revision.
How do I build a CommandHandler for an Alert aggregate in TypeScript? The handler should orchestrate readStream, decide, and appendToStream with optimistic concurrency.
Write a TypeScript CommandHandler for a Deployment aggregate using Effect.gen for IO. It must load via readStream, run decide, and appendToStream. Keep evolve and decide pure.
Build a TypeScript subscription runner for an Incident projection that resumes from the last stored checkpoint. Apply the event, then write the checkpoint — crash-safe order.
Write a TypeScript subscription that consumes an Alert stream and updates a read model. Persist the checkpoint only after the projection has been applied.
Implement a TypeScript projection runner for Renewal events that resumes from a checkpoint after a restart. Include the checkpoint-store interface and a runSubscription loop.
Write a generic aggregateStream helper in TypeScript and use it to fold a Release stream into its state. Use async iteration and two type parameters.
Use a when()-style reducer to build Membership state from events. Show a pure reduce<MembershipState> inside a helper that takes an event array.
Explain briefly how folding events rebuilds aggregate state, then implement aggregateStream for a Pipeline aggregate in TypeScript.
Write a when() reducer and an aggregate() helper for the Incident aggregate in TypeScript. Use reduce<IncidentState> with an exhaustive switch.
Write a TypeScript upcaster that migrates old Alert raw events to the current shape. Switch on { type, version } and throw UnknownEventError by default.
Implement an upcast() function for old Deployment events in TypeScript. Migrate DeploymentStarted v1 to v2 by filling in a default target, and throw on unknown shapes.
Write a TypeScript rebuild() that wipes and re-projects the Pipeline read model from revision 0 in batches. Accept from, batchSize, project, and persist options.
Build a TypeScript rebuild function for the Runbook read model. It reads events in batches, applies project, persists the batch, and supports resuming from a saved cursor.
Write a minimal CQRS boundary for the Release aggregate in TypeScript. handleCommand writes via decide + appendToStream; queryView reads from a read store. No shared state type.
Build a CQRS split for the Renewal aggregate in TypeScript. handleCommand uses the Decider and EventStore; queryView reads from the ViewStore.

Write a stateless Process Manager in TypeScript that reacts to Deployment events and emits follow-up commands: DeploymentSucceeded triggers NotifyOncall, DeploymentRolledBack triggers OpenIncident. react() returns null otherwise.

Implement a 3-step saga skeleton for an Incident flow in TypeScript: IncidentOpened triggers PageOncall, OncallPaged triggers StartMitigation, MitigationStarted triggers nothing. Pure react() with null returns.
Write a translate() function that converts Release domain events into versioned IntegrationEvent payloads. Some domain events should map to null (not every domain event is an integration event).

Implement a TypeScript outbox appender that transactionally persists Alert events and integration-outbox rows in one unit of work. Use a local transactional interface and describe the publisher's role in a comment.
Wrap a Pipeline CommandHandler in Effect.gen. Load the stream, decide, and appendToStream, all inside an Effect workflow. Keep evolve/decide pure.
Write an Effect.gen handler for an Incident command that uses appendToStream with expectedRevision and converts a concurrency error into a typed failure.
Refactor a Membership CommandHandler to use Effect.gen for IO while keeping evolve and decide as pure functions. The handler reads the stream, folds, decides, and appends with expectedRevision.
